title: cynometra ramiflora subsp. bijuga
intro: Cynometra ramiflora subsp. bijuga, also known as the Bijuga Cannonball Tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its bark, leaves, and seeds possessing medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cynometra ramiflora subsp. bijuga include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve boiling its bark, leaves, or seeds in water to make a tea or decoction that can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
